name: data_types
description: PIC clause, USAGE clause, COMP/COMP-3/COMP-1/COMP-2 formats, sign handling, storage size calculations, and numeric vs alphanumeric categories

name: file_handling
description: File I/O verbs (OPEN, CLOSE, READ, WRITE, REWRITE, DELETE, START), SELECT/ASSIGN, FD entries, file status codes, BLOCK CONTAINS, and RECORDING MODE

name: copybooks
description: COPY statement, REPLACING clause, pseudo-text, nested copybooks, DCLGEN includes, SYSLIB, and copybook library organization

name: conditional_logic
description: IF/ELSE/END-IF, EVALUATE/WHEN, 88-level condition names, relation/class/sign conditions, combined conditions with AND/OR/NOT, and abbreviated conditions

name: string_handling
description: STRING, UNSTRING, INSPECT (TALLYING/REPLACING/CONVERTING), reference modification, FUNCTION TRIM, and text parsing patterns

name: arithmetic
description: ADD, SUBTRACT, MULTIPLY, DIVIDE, COMPUTE statements, GIVING, ROUNDED, ON SIZE ERROR, REMAINDER, CORRESPONDING, decimal alignment, and intermediate results

name: table_handling
description: OCCURS clause, subscripts vs indexes, SEARCH and SEARCH ALL, INDEXED BY, OCCURS DEPENDING ON, multi-dimensional tables, and ASCENDING/DESCENDING KEY

name: paragraph_flow
description: PERFORM (inline, out-of-line, THRU, TIMES, UNTIL, VARYING), GO TO, fall-through, sections vs paragraphs, STOP RUN, GOBACK, and EXIT PARAGRAPH

name: io_processing
description: ACCEPT (FROM DATE/TIME/CONSOLE), DISPLAY, SORT statement with INPUT/OUTPUT PROCEDURE, MERGE, RELEASE, RETURN, and SORT-RETURN register

name: error_handling
description: FILE STATUS checking, DECLARATIVES/USE AFTER procedures, imperative error phrases (ON SIZE ERROR, ON OVERFLOW, ON EXCEPTION, AT END, INVALID KEY), and RETURN-CODE register

name: jcl_interaction
description: DD statement mapping to COBOL files, PARM field, RETURN-CODE and COND parameter, SYSIN/SYSOUT, STEPLIB/JOBLIB, concatenated datasets, and GDG basics

name: vsam
description: VSAM dataset types (KSDS, ESDS, RRDS), control intervals/areas, alternate indexes, CI/CA splits, and VSAM-specific file status codes

name: db2_embedded_sql
description: Static embedded SQL in COBOL: EXEC SQL, host variables, SQLCA/SQLCODE, cursors (DECLARE/OPEN/FETCH/CLOSE), indicator variables, WHENEVER, COMMIT/ROLLBACK

name: db2_dynamic_sql
description: Dynamic SQL in COBOL-DB2: EXECUTE IMMEDIATE, PREPARE/EXECUTE, SQLDA, parameter markers, cursor-based dynamic SQL, and dynamic WHERE clauses

name: cics
description: CICS online transaction processing: EXEC CICS commands, pseudo-conversational model, BMS maps, COMMAREA, channels/containers, TSQ, TDQ, and CICS error handling

name: batch_patterns
description: Batch design patterns: sequential processing loop, control break logic, master-transaction matching, checkpoint/restart, report generation, and accumulator patterns

name: cobol_structure
description: The four divisions (IDENTIFICATION, ENVIRONMENT, DATA, PROCEDURE), column layout (reference format), fixed vs free format, Area A vs Area B rules

name: working_storage
description: WORKING-STORAGE SECTION: level numbers, VALUE clause, REDEFINES, RENAMES (66), FILLER, group vs elementary items, SYNC, JUSTIFIED RIGHT, BLANK WHEN ZERO

name: subprograms
description: CALL statement, LINKAGE SECTION, BY REFERENCE/BY CONTENT/BY VALUE, static vs dynamic calls, nested programs, CANCEL, and ENTRY statement

name: report_writer
description: Report Writer module: RD entry, report groups (REPORT HEADING/FOOTING, PAGE HEADING/FOOTING, DETAIL, CONTROL), INITIATE, GENERATE, TERMINATE, SUM counters

name: debugging
description: Diagnosing COBOL failures: abend codes (S0C7, S0C4, S0CB), DISPLAY debugging, READY TRACE, USE FOR DEBUGGING, SYSUDUMP, compiler diagnostics, and common runtime errors

name: intrinsic_functions
description: Comprehensive catalog of COBOL intrinsic functions: mathematical, string, date/time, numeric conversion, and ordinal functions invoked with FUNCTION keyword

name: data_movement
description: MOVE statement rules, MOVE CORRESPONDING, INITIALIZE statement, SET statement, type conversion during moves, truncation/padding, de-editing, and justification rules

name: date_handling
description: Date processing: Gregorian/Julian formats, ACCEPT FROM DATE/DAY/TIME, FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE, date arithmetic with integer day numbers, century windowing, Y2K, leap year logic
